payload.summary: President Donald Trump officially nominated former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair on March 4, 2026, to succeed Jerome Powell. Warsh is characterized as 'rate-cut friendly,' signaling a potential shift toward more accommodative monetary policy under the new administration.
payload.market_impact: Expected downward pressure on the USD due to anticipated lower interest rates; generally bullish for commodities like gold and oil, though geopolitical tensions with Iran and Senate confirmation hurdles are currently causing volatility in yields.
